timeline.label: Q1 2026 interim report: Maine internal logging ramp continues; NB Adj EBITDA margin 30% vs Maine 9%; quarterly dividend C$0.29 maintained; 18,619,712 shares outstanding
FY2025: declared dividends C$1.16/sh while Free Cash Flow troughed at C$0.37/sh with zero carbon credit sales after C$24.6M sale in 2024
Established internal logging operations in Maine to address contractor capacity and trucking constraints
Acquired Maine harvesting and trucking assets for C$6.9M cash to support internal harvest transition
Sold 752,100 voluntary carbon credits for C$24.6M (Anew Katahdin Forestry Project, Maine easement); no repeat in FY2025
Renewed Sustainable Forestry Initiative certification for New Brunswick and Maine operations with no non-conformances
Malcolm Cockwell serving as interim CEO per Q1 2026 shareholder letter; permanent appointment pending human review
Conversion from income trust to corporation; quarterly dividend framework continues on TSX
IPO and TSX listing as Acadian Timber income fund; Macer Forest Holdings remains controlling shareholder (~52% with 100% DRIP)

industry.structure: Regional Northeast softwood and hardwood log markets with ~85 mill customers; Acadian is a major freehold owner in New Brunswick and Maine plus fee manager on ~1.33M Crown licensed acres. Pricing follows mill demand and fuel-adjustment mechanics; no national pricing power.
industry.share_shift: Acadian is gaining internal harvest share in Maine after contractor shortages; New Brunswick volumes shift between Crown services and freehold harvest depending on contractor availability and customer mix.
industry.trend: Weak lumber end-use markets and tariff-related hardwood pulp uncertainty persist in 2025–2026; voluntary carbon credits and real-estate or recreation leases remain episodic upside layers on a timber income base.
